analysis: Ablation with S = V_norm ** 2 and beta = 2.0. Result is 75.19%. This is lower than the pure visual baseline of 75.65% (which used S = V_norm and beta = 1.0). So artificially sharpening the margin via squaring and increasing beta actually hurts. It may be that the long-tailed nature of the dataset benefits from broad, soft repulsion against moderately similar classes rather than a strict focus only on the top-1 confusions.
